title: Deny by default is safer than allow on error
description: Parsing, policy lookup, network, cache, or configuration errors should not grant access to protected operations.
body: Use when an authorization path includes fallbacks, caches, feature flags, config fetches, or exception handling. Do not use to hide operational outages; denied results still need observability. Actions: keep failures as deny or fail-safe degraded mode, record diagnostic evidence, and avoid catch blocks that return allow. Validation signals include simulated policy errors producing deny. Risk boundary: allow-on-error in protected paths is a blocking security defect.
